title: Methods and agents that enhance myogenic progenitor cell engraftment
abstract: Disclosed herein are agents that enhance muscle stem cell engraftment, as well as methods and compositions using the same.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of increasing the engraftment efficiency of myogenic progenitor cells comprising contacting the myogenic progenitor cells with one or more agents that increase the engraftment efficiency of the myogenic progenitor cells into a subject. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the agent is selected from a compound that increases intracellular Ca 2+ levels in myogenic progenitor cells, meclofenamic acid (MFA), lysophosphatidic acid (LPA), and niflumic acid (NFA). 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the compound that increases intracellular Ca 2+ levels is a lipid. 4. The method of claims 1-3, wherein a concentration of about 0.1 to 150 uM NFA is contacted with the myogenic progenitor cells. 5. The method of claims 1-4, wherein a concentration of about 0.1-10 uM LPA is contacted with the myogenic progenitor cells. 6. The method of claims 1-5, wherein a concentration of about 1-50 uM MFA is contacted with the myogenic progenitor cells. 7. The method of claims 1-6, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are contacted with at least NFA and LPA. 8. The method of claims 1-7, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are contacted with one or more agents prior to transplant of the myogenic progenitor cells into a subject. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are contacted with the one or more agents at least one hour prior to transplant of the myogenic progenitor cells into the subject. 10. The method of claims 1-7, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are contacted with the agent in vivo. 11. The method of claims 1-10, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are endogenous to the subject. 12. The method of claims 1-11, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are derived from cells of the subject. 13. The method of claims 1-12, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells have been genetically modified to correct a genetic defect. 14. The method of claims 1-13, wherein the subject has a skeletal muscle condition or disease. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the skeletal muscle condition or disease is Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD). 16. The method of claims 1-15, wherein the subject has been or is being treated to increase endogenous progenitor muscle cell levels. 17. The method of claims 1-16, wherein the subject is human. 18. A method of enhancing tissue culture of muscle tissue, comprising adding one or more agents that increase the engraftment efficiency of myogenic progenitor cells into the muscle tissue. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the agent is selected from a compound that increases intracellular Ca 2+ levels in myogenic progenitor cells, meclofenamic acid (MFA), lysophosphatidic acid (LPA), and niflumic acid (NFA). 20. The method of claims 18-19, wherein the tissue culture comprises zebrafish cells or human cells. 21. A method of screening for a test agent that enhances engraftment of myogenic progenitor cells comprising contacting the test agent with a population of myogenic progenitor cells, adding the contacted myogenic progenitor cells to a muscle tissue, and comparing engraftment into the muscle tissue of the contacted myogenic cells to engraftment into the muscle tissue of control myogenic progenitor cells not contacted with the test agent, thereby determining whether the test agent enhances engraftment. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein at least one of the myogenic progenitor cells and the muscle tissue comprises a label. 23. The method of claim 21 or 22, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells and the muscle cells are zebrafish cells or human cells. 24. A composition comprising myogenic progenitor cells and one or more agents that enhances engraftment of the myogenic progenitor cells into muscle tissue. 25. The composition of claim 24, wherein the agent is selected from a compound that increases intracellular Ca 2+ levels in myogenic progenitor cells, meclofenamic acid (MFA), lysophosphatidic acid (LPA), and niflumic acid (NFA). 26. The composition of claim 24 or 25, wherein the one or more agents comprise NFA at a concentration of about 0.1 to 150 uM. 27. The composition of claims 24-26, wherein the one or more agents comprise LPA at a concentration of about 0.1-10 uM LPA. 28. The composition of claims 24-27, wherein the one or more agents comprise MFA at a concentration of about 1-50 uM MPA. 29. The composition of claims 24-28, wherein the one or more agents comprise NFA and LPA. 30. The composition of claims 24-29, wherein the myogenic progenitor cells are human myogenic progenitor cells. 31. A composition comprising one or more agents that increase the engraftment efficiency of myogenic progenitor cells into muscle tissue of a subject and a pharmaceutically acceptable diluent or excipient. 32. The composition of claim 31, wherein the agent is selected from a compound that increases intracellular Ca 2+ levels in myogenic progenitor cells, meclofenamic acid (MFA), lysophosphatidic acid (LPA), and niflumic acid (NFA).
